chapter_title: Section 10 Receivables Management and Cash Handling
snippet: Schools can receive revenue from several sources. This may involve invoicing families and sundry debtors as well as the receipt of cash/EFTPOS/BPAY. It is important that schools recognise this revenue appropriately. Cash receipts must be handled in a manner that minimises any associated risks. A receivable is money owing to the school from the provision of services or the sale of goods. It may be owed by families for items such as camps and excursions or third parties (sundry debtors) for items such as hire of school facilities. Comply with the Parent Payments policy (also known as parent voluntary contributions). Enter family and sundry debtor invoices into CASES21 in a timely manner using relevant general ledger and GST codes. Regularly review (preferably monthly) the sundry debtor balances to assess collectability and take any appropriate action.
